summary: Gruber reports on dramatic JavaScript and WebKit performance improvements in iPhone OS 2.0 compared to earlier versions, all running on the original iPhone hardware. Using Craig Hockenberry's benchmarks, he shows 2.0 is up to 22 times faster than 1.0.1 and up to 8 times faster than 1.1.4 in JavaScript execution. He emphasizes these gains come purely from software optimization, not new hardware. He argues the improvements matter beyond Safari because many native iPhone apps embed WebKit browsers, making the performance gains broadly beneficial across the platform.
key_points: iPhone OS 2.0 delivers massive JavaScript performance gains (up to 22x over 1.0.1) on the same original iPhone hardware, purely through software improvements
WebKit improvements benefit not just Safari but native apps like NetNewsWire, Twitterrific, and Instapaper that embed WebKit browsers
Despite the App Store launch excitement, most iPhone software at the time still ran in the web browser, making WebKit performance critical
The visible UI of Safari remained essentially unchanged — all the meaningful improvements were under the hood
money_quotes: But under the hood, MobileSafari 2.0's performance is hugely improved over 1.1.4.
This has nothing to do with the new iPhone 3G hardware — this is about dramatic performance improvements on original iPhones upgraded to the 2.0 OS.
Using MobileSafari simply feels faster, especially with web applications. Feel is by nature subjective, but JavaScript benchmarks back this up.
The results are obvious. WebKit JavaScript performance has improved steadily and significantly in just one year, with a huge jump between 1.1.4 and the new 2.0.0.
For all the hubbub regarding the new App Store, most 'iPhone software' runs in the web browser.
When WebKit performance improves, any app that uses WebKit improves, and WebKit improved a lot between iPhone 1.1.4 and 2.0.0.
key_insight: iPhone OS 2.0 delivered transformative WebKit performance gains through software alone, benefiting not just Safari but every native app that embeds a web view.
